articles.description: India’s growth story rests on a confident middle class and credible institutions, but a stark failure in consumer complaint redressal threatens both. Delayed justice, weak capacity and rising pendency could slow consumption and get in the way of our economic ambitions.

Congress awards Davanagere South ticket to Shamnur clan, stirs Muslim leaders’ anger
articles.description: The Congress party has named Samarth Shamnur as its candidate for the Davanagere South bypoll. This decision comes after intense lobbying from Muslim leaders. Shamnur is the grandson of the late Lingayat leader Shamnur Shivashankarappa. The party leadership held discussions with community leaders. The bypoll is scheduled for April 9. In Bagalkot, Umesh Meti was chosen as the candidate.

articles.title: Over half of countries have banned phones in schools: UNESCO

articles.title: How agriPV can turn India’s farms into dual-purpose powerhouses
articles.description: India’s ambitious energy transition goals — to have 300 GW of installed solar capacity by 2030 and achieve net-zero emissions by 2070 — put land at a premium. Utility-scale solar projects require large tracts of land while agriculture is already under pressure from competing land use. Agri-photovoltaics can ameliorate this conflict

articles.description: Amazon sees India as a key long-term investment. E-commerce is just beginning here, with significant room to grow. The company is investing billions more, focusing on AI, exports, and jobs. Amazon's network reaches all of India, ensuring speed and convenience. New air cargo routes are boosting services in the Northeast, benefiting customers and sellers alike.

articles.title: Amit Shah lauds Modi's record 8,931 days in public life
articles.description: Home Minister Amit Shah lauded Prime Minister Narendra Modi for becoming the longest-serving head of government in India, surpassing 8,931 days in public life. Shah highlighted Modi's dedication to nation-first governance, integrity, and tireless service, attributing the achievement to his unwavering commitment and the trust of the people.

articles.description: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis assured a high-level inquiry, monitored by the DGP, into the rape case involving self-styled godman Ashok Kharat. He stated no one will be spared and urged those with evidence to cooperate, while criticizing attempts to politicize the matter. Fadnavis also commented on political statements by Mamata Banerjee and Rahul Gandhi.

articles.description: Opposition MPs have submitted notices in Parliament seeking the removal of Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar. Charges include acting under executive influence and causing mass disenfranchisement through the SIR exercise. The notices cite seven grounds of proved misbehaviour. Opposition leaders are awaiting government action on these notices.

articles.description: For decades, potholes, filth and bad roads were dismissed as trivial irritants, unworthy of political attention. That may be changing. As middle-class frustration builds and civic anger spills into public debate, the quality of life could actually matter at the ballot booth.

Modi ji and Shah ji are attempting to do 'khela' in West Bengal, and the value of the rupee is declining...Modi ji was saying that when the value of the rupee falls, the country's prestige falls. What is it which is falling now?...," he said.

A mobile phone deposit facility will also be available. These measures aim for a smooth voting experience.

Companies are sourcing cylinders, using firewood, and adjusting menus while avoiding disruptions. Facility managers are preparing contingency plans, including shifting to induction and alternative equipment, amid ongoing uncertainty over fuel availability and supply continuity.

articles.description: Manipur Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh held a meeting with Kuki group representatives in Guwahati as a confidence-building measure amid ongoing ethnic tensions in the state. The meeting comes against the backdrop of past and recent clashes involving Kuki, Meitei and Naga communities, including the 2023 violence that led to significant casualties.

articles.title: Pushkar Dhami expands cabinet with 5 ministers, signals stability ahead of Uttarakhand polls
articles.description: Uttarakhand's cabinet expansion saw five new ministers inducted, bringing the total to 12, aiming to balance caste and regional representation ahead of next year's Assembly elections. This reshuffle, likely the last for the Dhami government, seeks to counter anti-incumbency and solidify leadership support, dispelling rumors of a change.

articles.title: UBT Sena targets Eknath Shinde after Rupali Chakankar resigns over Ashok Kharat row
articles.description: Maharashtra politics heats up after a state women's commission chief resigns due to her association with a spiritual leader accused of rape. The Uddhav Balasaheb Thackeray Sena now targets Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde. Leaders demand action against political followers of such spiritual figures. The Sena alleges black magic was used to destabilize a previous government.

articles.title: Manipur CM meets Kuki groups in Guwahati; security forces recover IEDs, arms across state
articles.description: Manipur Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh met with Kuki groups in Guwahati. This meeting aimed to build confidence between communities. Security forces have recovered numerous weapons and explosives. Recoveries include IEDs, rifles, and pistols from multiple districts. These actions follow recent clashes and highlight ongoing security concerns in the region.

articles.title: Supreme Court concerned over cops uploading videos online, says poses threat to fair trial
articles.description: The senior counsel flagged the recent trend of police posting the images of accused persons being handcuffed, tied by ropes, paraded, forced to kneel, etc, on social media, which, apart from causing an affront to personal dignity, adds to the public bias.

articles.title: BJP readies Uttar Pradesh cabinet reshuffle ahead of polls
articles.description: With Uttarakhand having already seen a cabinet reshuffle, Uttar Pradesh is next as internal BJP discussions gain momentum ahead of the 2026 Assembly elections. A key meeting at Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath’s residence, attended by senior leaders including RSS joint general secretary Arun Kumar, signalled active planning within the party.

articles.title: Zetwerk IPO: Size, valuation, timeline and other details we know so far

The company reported over 1,212 fake websites between April 1, 2024 and January 5, 2026 and said that it is cooperating with authorities. CoinDCX also warned of rising cyber fraud in India’s digital finance ecosystem.

The suspended legislators are accused of violating party discipline. The BJD plans to seek their disqualification from the Assembly.

Space-based data centres could reduce costs by not relying on solar energy, avoiding land use, and not depending on local power grids, it said. However, the move comes after Amazon’s satellite internet division, Amazon Leo, told the US Federal Communications Commission to reject SpaceX’s plan to deploy up to one million satellites as orbiting data centres.

Political parties were present during this transparent allocation of voting machines.

articles.description: Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan has accused the Congress and Rahul Gandhi of acting as the Bharatiya Janata Party's B-team. He criticized their appeasement politics and support-seeking from communal groups. Vijayan also defended the probe into the Sabarimala gold loss issue, stating the High Court found no fault.

He stated the ongoing war's damage to combatants, the region, and India has exceeded acceptable limits. Tharoor called for an immediate end to the hostilities. He urged India to lead international efforts for de-escalation. The conflict's impact on ordinary lives and rising oil prices were highlighted.

articles.title: Amazon acquires Swiss robotics company Rivr to test robots for doorstep delivery
articles.description: The acquisition of the Zurich-based autonomous robotics startup took place earlier this week, but the terms of the deal have not been disclosed. Earlier, Amazon had participated in Rivr’s 2024 seed funding round. To date, Rivr has raised a total of $25 million and was last valued at around $100 million. Rivr has developed a four-legged robot mounted on wheels, which was described by cofounder and CEO Marko Bjelonic as a “dog on roller skates."

articles.title: Delhi Court grants time to Mallikarjun Kharge to file reply in revision against dismissal of complaint
articles.description: A Delhi court has given Congress Chief Mallikarjun Kharge more time to respond to a revision petition. This petition challenges the earlier rejection of a complaint alleging hate speech by Kharge during a Karnataka election rally. The trial court had previously dismissed the complaint, stating no offense was made out. The matter is now scheduled for further hearing next month.

articles.title: Satara Zilla Parishad setback: Minister warns BJP of fallout after Sena-NCP defeat in future polls
articles.description: The Shiv Sena has accused the BJP of undermining democracy after losing the Satara Zilla Parishad president election. Despite the Shiv Sena-NCP combine holding a majority, the BJP secured the top post. Shiv Sena minister Shamhuraj Desai vowed to initiate disqualification proceedings against defecting members. He warned of severe consequences for the BJP in future elections.

articles.title: Pinterest CEO calls for ban on social media for youth under-16
articles.description: Pinterest CEO Bill Ready ​called on world leaders to ban social media for youth under 16 in a LinkedIn post on Friday. Ready posted his statement while ‌a trial ⁠is under ⁠way in Los Angeles about youth social media use. Google and ​Meta face allegations that their apps are fueling a youth mental health crisis. ​The jury is deliberating on a verdict.

articles.title: US jury finds Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders
articles.description: A federal jury in California found Friday that tech titan Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders in an effort to drive down the company's share price as he was poised to buy it in a $44 billion deal. The civil complaint accused Musk of driving down Twitter's stock price to gain leverage to renegotiate the purchase price or get out of the deal completely, causing people who sold shares to lose money.

articles.title: KP Singh: The visionary who turned Gurgaon into corporate India’s hub
